persistent_brief: This is an android application capable of pregnancy diet plan. Were you can give it what you eat and it gives back if you took the right nutrition and other details.
project_summary: Android Kotlin/Jetpack Compose pregnancy diet tracker using Firebase Auth and Firestore. The app tracks onboarding/profile data, symptoms, supplements, meals, weight, deterministic nutrition summaries, AI insights, reminders, reports, settings/privacy controls, and signed GitHub APK releases for sideload testing.
architecture_notes: Layered packages remain split across ui/screens, viewmodels/features, data interfaces, firebase repositories, model classes, nutrition logic, ai provider/request parsing, safety rules, and reminder scheduling.

AI insights are generated through the AI repository/provider abstraction, validated by safety guardrails, embedded back into source Firestore documents, and also logged deterministically under users/{uid}/aiSummaries/{summaryId}.

Daily and weekly AI insight flows persist AI nutrition estimates to their summary documents with aiNutritionProcessed, nutritionProcessedBy, and nutritionProcessingStatus markers; reusable AI-processed summaries skip repeat provider calls for the same date or ISO week.
GitHub sideload releases now publish a signed APK and document that older debug-signed builds may need to be uninstalled because Android rejects updates signed by a different certificate.
quality_focus: Use ANDROID_HOME=/opt/android-sdk for local validation because local.properties does not define sdk.dir in this environment.
GitHub release v0.1.5 publishes app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk as a signed APK asset; do not publish unsigned APKs as installable assets.
Older v0.1.0-v0.1.3 debug APKs used a different debug signing certificate than v0.1.4/v0.1.5; same-package Android updates across that boundary require uninstalling the older sideloaded app first.
open_followups: Consider invalidating or versioning cached daily/weekly AI summaries if users edit meals, symptoms, supplements, or profile inputs after an AI-processed summary has been saved.
Move GitHub release signing to a private, stable release keystore before distributing beyond internal/manual testing.

recent_requests.prompt: Consider the current code. I noticed that the ai summary is not being saved or logged anywhere. Let's log this to firestore as well for future analysis. Also local analysis of nutrition is not really important. Instead I want you to make the nutrition obtained from the AI saved and identified that it has been processed by the AI. So that it does not need to be processed again if summary is requested again during the day or week.
Check the code builds and generate the apk and publish it to git.
You did not release the app to github
The new release just says app not installed and nothing happens. Fix it and release again to git.

last_task.log_excerpt: Fixed the GitHub sideload packaging by bumping to v0.1.5/versionCode 6, lowering minSdk to API 23, making release APKs signed instead of unsigned, documenting the legacy debug-signature update conflict, and validating unit tests plus debug/release APK builds before publishing the new release.
